data.name: Tara Nadal
data.bio:
Excited to be beginning to the Doctoral Program in Catechetics this year at Franciscan.  I completed the MACE program in 2020 and the M.A, in Catholic Studies program in 2023.  What can I say?  I love being an online student at Franciscan.

I am a musician, catechist, choir and concert band director and high school religion and music teacher in Brampton, Ontario, Canada. In the evenings, I work as a lay pastoral associate at St. John the Evangelist parish in the Archdiocese of Toronto.  I am also entering my 3rd year as an aspirant for the Ordo Virginum for the Archdiocese of Toronto and am hoping to be consecrated in the next few years!

data.name: Erika Sison
data.bio:
My name is Erika Sison and I am from Wellington, New Zealand. I will be studying a Masters of Theological Studies at Franciscan University of Steubenville, and am very excited to learn more about our beautiful Catholic faith. It has been a dream of mine to study Theology at Franciscan University since I was a teenager, and it has been a desire that I have carried in my heart for more than 10 years and has not wavered so I truly believe that this desire is something that the Lord has placed in my heart. I felt God calling me to step out in faith and apply to study in 2025. I hope that through the Theological Studies program, that I can know, love and serve God more in wherever He leads me. I am particularly interested in Theology of the Body and am passionate about marriage and family. 

Some of my interests and hobbies in my spare time include going on walks and hikes, reading, watching musicals and going to daily mass and adoration when I can. I love our Catholic faith and I love our Beloved Lord, and cannot wait to learn alongside you all. Please pray for me as I begin studies as a full-time online student at Franciscan University.

In Christ
Erika

data.name: Lloyd Louks
data.bio:
Hello good people!

My name is Lloyd and I am a somewhat sarcastic spiritual sleuth, packing my faith along the path unto the mystery of understanding God. I have felt the hand of God guide me all my life, although I didn't always recognize it, and sometimes reacted appropriately to His directions - especially when He yells, "Duck!" I am in the Order of St. Francis as a Secular Franciscan and serve as the Vice Minister of the Sacred Heart Fraternity, Indianapolis, Indiana. I've been a parishioner at St. Barnabas Church for many years, served on committees and parish council, and sing in the choir.

This humble penitent would like to serve the Lord as a Chaplain, praying with and consoling the sick, lame, and even the healthy that just need to hear the love of God. A new career as a military chaplain would be tons of fun, especially in the U.S. Navy, but that might be a stretch in their age requirements. A career as a chaplain in a VA Medical Center or civilian hospital would also work.

I pray that this semester is productive for us all and that all our assignments are completed on time.

Peace and all good.

Lloyd Louks, OFS
317-721-1805 Mobile

data.name: Brian Young
data.bio: I'm an older student, retired Military, retired stockbroker and retired teacher.  I served 10 years active duty Army from the mid 80's to the mid 90's, got out and joined the Reserves while starting my civilian job as a Financial Consultant with a major brokerage firm.  Just when I'm getting used to the job, 9/11 happens and I begin a series of Active Duty Army assignments overseas.  From Jan 2002 to 2014 I deployed about 4 times.  I retired from both jobs around 2016 and became a JROTC high school Senior Army Instructor, first in South Korea then just outside Savannah, GA and retired from that after 3 years.  Golf became my primary activity in retirement and soon realized how I wanted to dive into my faith.  I was educated in Catholic schools up to 9th grade, and everything after that has been a lot of reading and participating (like teaching catechism to middle schoolers) in the activities in my parish.

data.bio: Originally from Iowa City (IA), Ben has lived in the Western Des Moines area since 2013. Ben holds BA (Philosophy), MA (Philosophy), and JD degrees conferred by the University of Iowa. Baptized Lutheran as an infant, Ben later sought out and was later confirmed by the Episcopalian Church of America at the age of 30. When not playing video games with his eleven-year-old son (Will), Ben enjoys reading, writing, and catching sunshine in his convertible, the pool, on Burntside Lake (MN), or any tropical location he can.

data.name: Anthony Digmann
data.bio:
Anthony is a husband and father, speaker, author, Catholic educator, and a Passionately Catholic YouTuber. He has been featured on international Catholic media such as EWTN, Catholic Digest, and Radio Maria, as well as various national Catholic radio stations, podcasts, blogs, and diocesan periodicals.

Anthony holds Master of Arts degrees in Catechetics and Evangelization from Franciscan University of Steubenville and in Theology from Loras College. He is currently in the inaugural cohort pursuing a Doctor of Ministry in Evangelization and Catechetics at Franciscan University. He has served the Church professionally in Catholic schools, parish ministry, and on councils of the Archdiocese of Dubuque since 2006.

Anthony is the author of 5 books and 3 journals sold on 4 continents covering topics such as discipleship, prayer, marriage, Star Wars, kerygma and how to respond to the Gospel, and spiritual warfare related to demons and exorcism. Find Anthony’s books on Amazon.

Since 2008 Anthony has served on the Theology faculty at Beckman Catholic High School and has instructed college courses since 2012 for Saint Louis University, Mount Mercy University, and Northeast Iowa Community College.

One unique fact about Anthony is that he has a father for a brother and a brother for a father–in a completely licit Catholic way, of course. His father is a consecrated religious Brother, and his brother is a priest for the Archdiocese of Dubuque.

Anthony is a professional videographer and disc golf course designer as well. He travels from Dyersville, Iowa, the home of the St. Francis Xavier Basilica and the Field of Dreams.

data.name: Scott Burns
data.bio: I am married to Jennifer and live in The Woodlands, Texas.  We have four kids ranging in ages from 18 to 23.  I would at a big energy company in Houston and spent a decade as an Air Force pilot before moving into business.  We are officially empty nesters and I'm working toward my Masters of Theology and Christian Ministry degree in anticipation of retiring in several years and spending more time in outreach ministries.  I'm active in ACTS retreats, Kolbe prison retreats, our parish's food pantry, our parish Honduras mission, and was in our St. Vincent de Paul Society up until recently.  I think these service ministries are a great way to demonstrate our love for others without coming across as zealous or judgmental.

data.name: Vinicius Almeida da Silva
data.bio:
Hello, everyone! My name is Vinícius K. Almeida da Silva, and I am a Brazilian Catholic seminarian. I am 25 years old and currently live in the city of Itumbiara, Goiás, Brazil.  I am in the final stage of formation, preparing for diaconal ordination (which, by God´s grace, will take place this year). This is my first semester in the graduate theology at Franciscan University. Everything is very new to me, especially because the American academic system is significantly different from the Brazilian one.  Moreover, although I understand English (as a foreign language), it is the first time I have to complete academic-level work in this beautiful yet challenging language. 

I hold a bachelor´s degree in Philosophy (2020) and Theology (2024), and a Specialization in Classical Education (2024). My choice to attend Fransiscan University was initially due to my admiration for the writings and apologetic work of professor Dr. Scott Hahn and other professors from the Saint Paul Center. When Dr. Hahn visited Brazil for the last time, I had the opportunity to attend one of his passionate lectures and speak with him privately with a few minutes. It was after this encounter that I discovered where he taught and that this very university offered a distance Master´s program – exactly what I needed at that time. God´s providence is always wonderful. My enthusiasm for this program only grew as I researched the faculty and the pedagogical approach. I feel deeply honored to be a student in this program.

data.name: John Pace
data.bio:
My name is John Pace and I am brand new to Franciscan University of Steubenville! 

I currently live in York, PA and work in Harrisburg, PA for the Tribunal of the Catholic Diocese of Harrisburg. I am married to a wonderful woman and have two wonderful sons, one a senior and the other a sophomore. I also have three awesome step children and three grandchildren. I am very exited for all my children as they discern their career paths, especially for my older son Benjamin who is currently in the application process to enter the seminary.

For over twenty years, I had an awesome and successful career in aviation, as an aviation human factors engineer and commercial pilot. Recently, however, I felt that the Lord was calling me to do something radically different with my life- "to take a leap-of-faith". So after much prayer, reflection, and thought, I feel that the Lord has called me to become a Canon Lawyer. Canon Law is a topic which has always interested and fascinated me from many many years ago when I myself was in the seminary. So, long story short, I am here to take some pre-requisite masters level Theology courses before I enter the School of Canon Law at Catholic University of America in Washington, D.C. 

I will not be matriculating for a degree, and may be here a shorter time than many classmates however, I look forward to fulling embracing the FUS experience. Please be assured of my prayers for you as I ask for your prayers for me. And, as always thanks be to God for ALL things.

data.name: John Hernandez
data.bio:
My name is John Hernandez, and I live in Lubbock, Texas, with my wife, our two dogs, and our cat. I work as a Retail Associate Manager for T‑Mobile, and I hold an Associate of Science degree from South Plains College in Levelland.

My wife and I serve as catechists and Confirmation coordinators at Our Lady of Grace Catholic Church, where we have a deep passion for forming young disciples. I’m currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in theology to deepen my understanding of the faith and to better serve others as both a catechist and, God willing, a future deacon in our parish.

Outside of ministry and studies, I enjoy watching football and cartoons, and I’m a proud Detroit Lions fan.

data.name: April Schroeder
data.bio:
I am a wife and mother of eight energetic, strong-willed, and charitable children.  I have home-schooled for the past 18 years, and continue to do so.  My oldest is 23 and my youngest is 8.  
I have been a teacher of religion and finance at the local Catholic middle school.  I have taught CCD for the past 17 years and began the youth ministry for our parish.
I am a lay Dominican and was finally professed in 2010.  I feel called to the Dominican charisms of preaching, study, prayer, and community,
I have a Bachelors degree in Business Administration with a double major in corporate financial management and personal financial planning.
My occupation is a homemaker and it is the best job in the world!  I am blest by God to do what I love, and what I was created by Him to do - "My cup runneth over" (Psalm 23:5).

data.name: Stephen Estes
data.bio:
Disciple of Christ, husband to Christina, and father to our daughter, Clelia Rose. 

I am currently the Director of Faith Formation and Discipleship at the Archdiocese of St. Louis and oversee a shared program with the Augustine Institute offering Certificates in Evangelization to lay leaders. I also continue to lead an Evangelization & Catechetics Certification program for the Diocese of Houma-Thibodaux. I was raised in southern Illinois, studied Psychology at the University of Missouri and then obtained a Master's in Theology from FUS. I have been a college campus minister, theology teacher, Director of Missions for Life Teen, and a Pastoral Consultant with the Diocese of Houma-Thibodaux.

My family lives in St. Louis but also maintains a residence in New Orleans, LA.

data.name: Dominic Drews
data.bio:
Hello!
I am a 22 year old middle school youth minister in Denton, Texas.

I've done a little bit of everything:
I went through a fire academy and was a certified firefighter/EMT, thinking that was the path I would be taking in life, only for God to have other (much better) plans.

I also went to film school and got an associates degree in "Motion Picture Production" which is just fancy talk for "Film."

I still love filmmaking and feel called to share God through the medium of film, though right now, I feel called to higher education in Psychology and Theology, to perhaps become a therapist. I don't always know what I'm doing, so when I feel like God is calling me one way or another, I try to have as little hesitation as possible to take a leap of faith.

In my free time, I make movies, watch movies, fly drones (I'm a commercial drone pilot), play piano (though I'm very rusty), write stories/screenplays, ride motorcycles, hang out with friends/family, and much more.
(Sometimes I get told that I have too many hobbies)

If you are taking the time to read all of this, I'm genuinely impressed and thank you for having an interest in me. Please pray for me, as we are all in need!
